source.title: Laboratory Evolution of Fast-Folding Green Fluorescent Protein Using Secretory Pathway Quality Control

initial_request.research_problem: GFP folding inefficiency in E. coli; existing variants (frGFP, sfGFP) show limited fluorescence difference in cytoplasm, hindering directed evolution.
initial_request.known_context: Previous folding-enhanced variants (frGFP, sfGFP) showed only a 65% increase in geometric mean fluorescence in cytoplasm, insufficient for FACS-based directed evolution.
constraints: Must use SecYEG secretory pathway quality control for selection
quantifiable_goals: Achieve large fluorescence difference between folded and unfolded variants when targeted to SecYEG
Isolate superfolded variants with improved folding kinetics

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Cytoplasmic expression of frGFP and sfGFP showed only a 65% increase in geometric mean fluorescence, insufficient for FACS-based directed evolution.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Insufficient fluorescence difference in cytoplasm to discriminate folding variants.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: SecYEG-mediated quality control can discriminate folding and be used to engineer superfolded proteins.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Test targeting of sfGFP and frGFP to SecYEG via different signal peptides.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Hypothesis formulated that native cellular quality control pathways can exert evolutionary pressure to improve protein folding.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Need to experimentally validate that SecYEG targeting yields large fluorescence differences.

evidence.quote_or_span: there was only a small increase (65%) in the geometric mean fluorescence of cells expressing sfGFP relative to frGFP

we explored the hypothesis that native cellular quality control pathways exert sufficient evolutionary pressure to improve protein folding properties beyond those that are normally sufficient for solubility and function within a cell.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Hypothesis that SecYEG quality control can discriminate folding.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Unknown whether SecYEG targeting yields sufficient fluorescence difference between folded and unfolded GFP variants.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Targeting sfGFP and frGFP to SecYEG via signal peptides will result in large fluorescence differences due to differential folding and degradation.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Construct fusions of sfGFP and frGFP with ssDsbA, ssMBP, ssPhoA and measure fluorescence.
evolution_trajectory.observation: 67-, 47-, and 24-fold greater fluorescence for ssDsbA-sfGFP, ssMBP-sfGFP, ssPhoA-sfGFP relative to corresponding frGFP fusions.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Large fluorescence differences observed; need to understand subcellular localization.

evidence.quote_or_span: we observed a 67-, 47- and 24-fold greater fluorescence emission for cells expressing ssDsbA-sfGFP, ssMBP-sfGFP and ssPhoA-sfGFP fusions, respectively, relative to the corresponding frGFP fusions

Upon induced expression of each fusion, we observed a 67-, 47- and 24-fold greater fluorescence emission for cells expressing ssDsbA-sfGFP, ssMBP-sfGFP and ssPhoA-sfGFP fusions, respectively, relative to the corresponding frGFP fusions (shown in Fig.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Large fluorescence differences observed with SecYEG targeting.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: sfGFP accumulates in cytoplasm due to rapid folding, while frGFP is degraded.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Perform subcellular fractionation to localize fluorescence.
evolution_trajectory.observation: sfGFP fusions localized predominantly in cytoplasmic fraction; ssDsbA-frGFP efficiently degraded and does not accumulate.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Confirms folding-dependent discrimination; test GFPmut2 as baseline for selection.

evidence.quote_or_span: subcellular fractionation revealed that the fluorescence emitted by cells expressing the sfGFP fusions was localized predominantly in the cytoplasmic fraction
ssDsbA-frGFP is efficiently degraded and does not accumulate anywhere in cells

evolution_trajectory.state_before: sfGFP accumulates in cytoplasm, frGFP degraded; need a low baseline for selection.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Need a starting variant with low fluorescence when targeted to SecYEG.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: GFPmut2 targeted to SecYEG will show low fluorescence, providing a baseline for evolution.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Target GFPmut2 to SecYEG via ssDsbA and compare to ssDsbA-sfGFP.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Cells expressing ssDsbA-GFPmut2 were 46-fold less fluorescent than ssDsbA-sfGFP.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Low baseline confirmed; test if rapid folding can trap protein in cytoplasm via TrxA fusion.

evidence.quote_or_span: cells expressing GFPmut2 targeted to SecYEG via ssDsbA were 46-fold less fluorescent than cells expressing ssDsbA-sfGFP
However, cells expressing GFPmut2 targeted to SecYEG via ssDsbA were 46-fold less fluorescent than cells expressing ssDsbA-sfGFP (Fig.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: SecYEG targeting provides large fluorescence difference; GFPmut2 is a suitable baseline.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Need to generate diversity for directed evolution.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Error-prone PCR of GFPmut2 fused to DsbA signal peptide will create a library of variants with varying folding properties.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Construct error-prone PCR library of GFPmut2 fused to ssDsbA.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Library constructed with high error rate.

evidence.quote_or_span: Libraries of GFPmut2 were synthesized using the mutagenic method of Fromant et al. [45] and inserted in-frame with the coding region for the DsbA signal peptide.
We cloned a high-rate error-prone DNA library (4.8% amino acid error) of GFPmut2 variants downstream of ssDsbA resulting in a diverse

evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Need to isolate variants with improved folding.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: FACS screening will enrich for variants with high fluorescence due to cytoplasmic accumulation.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Perform two rounds of FACS on library cells.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Isolated 9 colonies (P1-P9) with fluorescent phenotypes above continuum distribution.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Colonies isolated; sequence to identify mutations.

evidence.quote_or_span: Following two rounds of FACS we isolated 9 colonies that displayed fluorescent phenotypes significantly above the continuum distribution.
3,023 colonies were recovered, scraped, and inoculated into a fresh culture for a second round of FACS.
1×10 $^{6}$ DH5 $\alpha$ library cells (diversity $\sim$ 1.5×10 $^{6}$ ) were subjected to FACS.

evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Need to identify mutations responsible for improved fluorescence.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Sequencing will reveal recurring substitutions that enhance folding.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Identified 7 recurring substitutions: N105Y, F64L, E124V, K101E, A206V, G232D, V163A out of 35 total substitutions.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Mutations identified; validate that they confer increased fluorescence.

evidence.quote_or_span: Of the 35 total substitutions that were found, 7 specific substitutions occurred more than once and accounted for 19 of the 35 total substitutions. These recurring substitutions with frequencies in parenthesis were: N105Y (4×), F64L (4×), E124V (3×), K101E (2×), A206V (2×), G232D (2×) and V163A (2×).
These recurring substitutions with frequencies in parenthesis were: N105Y (4×), F64L (4×), E124V (3×), K101E (2×), A206V (2×), G232D (2×) and V163A (2×).
Some of the substitutions even appeared in combination multiple times, for example N105Y/E124V (3×) and even K101E/N105Y/E124V/G232D (2×).

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Variants P1-P9 identified with recurring substitutions.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Need to confirm that increased fluorescence is due to the mutations and not artifacts.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Re-transformation into fresh cells will reproduce increased fluorescence and cytoplasmic accumulation.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Re-transform plasmids P1-P9 into fresh cells and measure fluorescence and localization.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Re-transformed cells showed increased fluorescence relative to ssDsbA-GFPmut2 and cytoplasmic accumulation.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Validation successful; test if variants retain fluorescence without signal peptide.

evidence.quote_or_span: we first re-transformed the recovered plasmids (clones P1–P9) into fresh cells and observed that re-transformed cells expressing variants P1–P9 all showed increased fluorescence relative to ssDsbA-GFPmut2 (Fig. 2a) as a result of cytoplasmic accumulation of each variant (Fig. 1d, e).

evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Need to confirm that folding improvement is intrinsic and not dependent on signal peptide.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Removing signal peptide will not significantly reduce fluorescence if folding is improved.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Subclone variants without ssDsbA and measure fluorescence.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Little to no loss in fluorescence compared to parental GFPmut2, indicating folding improvement without functional loss.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Folding improvement confirmed; characterize selected variants in detail.

evidence.quote_or_span: We subcloned the variants appended with a C-terminal 6x-histidine tag but without the coding region for ssDsbA and observed that cells expressing these signal sequence-less GFPmut2 variants showed little to no loss in fluorescence activity when compared to the parental GFPmut2 protein (Fig. 2b), indicating that any improvement in GFP folding (see below) was accomplished without significantly affecting the proteins' normal function.

We subcloned the variants appended with a C-terminal 6x-histidine tag but without the coding region for ssDsbA and observed that cells expressing these signal sequence-less GFPmut2 variants showed little to no loss in fluorescence activity when compared to the parental GFPmut2 protein (Fig.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Variants retain fluorescence without signal peptide.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Need to characterize folding properties of best variants.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: P4, P5, P7 will show improved folding kinetics.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Purify P4, P5, P7 and measure soluble yield, fluorescence, and refolding kinetics.
evolution_trajectory.observation: P4, P5, P7 had slightly lower soluble yield and fluorescence; P4 and P5 refolding comparable to sfGFP, P7 far faster; P7 renamed superfast GFP.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Refolding kinetics characterized; examine unfolding kinetics to understand mechanism.

evidence.quote_or_span: Following purification, we observed that P4, P5, and P7 had a slightly lower soluble yield and slightly lower total fluorescence relative to GFPmut2 (Table 1)

the refolding speed of variants P4 and P5 was comparable to, while P7 far eclipsed, that of sfGFP (Fig. 3a, Table 1). Since sfGFP was one of the fastest folding GFPs to date [7], we renamed P7 'superfast' GFP.

Following purification, we observed that P4, P5, and P7 had a slightly lower soluble yield and slightly lower total fluorescence relative to GFPmut2 (Table 1), suggesting that these clones represent a novel solution to protein folding optimization as neither the function nor the soluble yield of the protein-of-interest was improved.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: P4, P5, P7 show improved refolding kinetics.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Need to understand if variants were selected for folding kinetics or stability.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Kinetic unfolding will reveal if variants have increased stability.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Measure unfolding kinetics of superfast GFP, P5, P4.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Superfast GFP and P5 unfold very slowly; P4 unfolds like GFPmut2. Variants isolated primarily on improved folding kinetics.

evidence.quote_or_span: we examined the kinetics of unfolding and discovered that superfast GFP and P5 unfolded very slowly whereas P4 unfolded at a speed comparable to GFPmut2 (Fig. 3c, Table 1). Thus, while we conclude that each of these variants appears to have been isolated primarily based on improved folding kinetics, laboratory evolution returned three unique responses to the pressure imposed by secretory quality control

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Variants P4, P5, and superfast GFP characterized with improved folding kinetics.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Need to confirm that the overall approach successfully yielded superfolded clones.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: A single round of mutagenesis and two rounds of FACS are sufficient to yield superfolded clones.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Summarize the outcome of the directed evolution experiment.
evolution_trajectory.observation: A single round of mutagenesis and two rounds of FACS yielded superfolded clones P4, P5, and superfast GFP that each exhibited significant folding-enhancement relative to the parental GFPmut2 protein.

evidence.quote_or_span: a single round of mutagenesis and two rounds of FACS yielded superfolded clones P4, P5, and superfast GFP that each exhibited significant folding-enhancement relative to the parental GFPmut2 protein.

validation_methods: Re-transformation of plasmids into fresh cells
Protein purification and kinetic refolding/unfolding assays
success_verification.final_conclusion: A single round of mutagenesis and two rounds of FACS yielded superfolded clones P4, P5, and superfast GFP that each exhibited significant folding-enhancement relative to the parental GFPmut2 protein.
limitations: Need to optimize promoter/induction conditions to avoid saturating quality control machinery.
